content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

March 30, 1977

Jane Shuttleworth Wiegand
City Attorney
100 Valley Blvd.
Escondido, California 92025

Dear City Attorney Wiegand:

This is in response to your request for an opinion dated March 28, 1977. Your letter will not be treated as a request for an opinion but rather as a request for staff advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329. Your letter raises no substantial question under the Political Reform Act. You indicate that a city councilman who is a disc jockey-newscaster on radio station, KOWN, owned by Palomar Broadcasters, Inc. may be asked to vote on matters involving the Anderson Company, a land developer and an advertiser with KOWN. The issue before the Council concerns the approval of a tentative subdivision map of the company.

Government Code Section 87100 provides: "[no public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest." Financial interest has been defined as follows:

"87103. Financial Interest. An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value received by or promised to the public official within twelve months prior to the time when the decision is made; or

(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management."

Jane S. Wiegand
page two
March 30, 1977


     Assuming that the decision before the city council will have a
material financial effect upon Dickerson, Councilman Best must have
an interest in Dickerson before disqualification is required of him.
We have no information which would lead us to believe that Councilman
Best has an investment in or position of management with the Dickerson
company. Likewise based upon the representations, above, it appears
that Councilman Best does not have the Dickerson company as a source
of income to him. As related in your letter Mr. Best will receive no
money directly or indirectly from the Dickerson account and Mr. Best
does not have a ten percent interest or greater in KQWN or Palomar
Broadcasters, Inc. (See Government Code Sections 82030 and 87207(b)(2)).
Furthermore, I assume that Councilman has no interest in real property
which may be affected by the pending decision of the Council. Based
upon the foregoing it is my conclusion that Mr. Best may participate
in decisions of the Escondido City Council on matters related to the
Dickerson subdivision map. If I may be of further assistance to you
in this matter, please feel free to contact me at 322-6444 in Sacramento.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mfa

JANE SHUTTLEWORTH WIEGAND
CITY ATTORNEY
ELROY F. WIEHL
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

CITY OF ESCONDIDO
100 VALLEY BLVD., ESCONDIDO,
CALIFORNIA 92025
(714) 741-4608

March 28, 1977


Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
Chairman
The Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Dear Chairman Lowenstein:

The purpose of this letter is to request an opinion regarding the legality of a City Councilman's participation in a governmental decision relating to a client of his employer, as more specifically described hereinbelow.

The radio station, KOWN, is an AM-FM station, owned by Palomar Broadcasters, Inc. It employs Mr. Douglas Best, Escondido City Councilman, as an Account Executive, Newscaster and Disc Jockey. Individuals and firms having accounts with KOWN appear from time to time before the Escondido City Council on matters which entail a vote of the members of said Council. The factual situation which occasions this request for an opinion involves the Dickerson Company, a land development company. Said company has a Tentative Subdivision Map relating to a 61 lot residential subdivision on a 16 acre site in Escondido. The City Council was asked by said company to approve the Tentative Map. Shortly before said councilmanic action the Dickerson Company had bought time at KOWN and desired that Mr. Best broadcast the commercials. Mr. Best's employer, KOWN, receives a fee for airing the commercial. Mr. Best does not receive any money directly from the Dickerson account, having declined to take the commission resulting from his acquisition of said account.

Mr. Best describes his remuneration at KOWN as follows:

"I receive a monthly draw against commissions. If I do not sell sufficient time to meet the draw I make nothing in excess of the draw, except a guarantee I have for doing three newscasts per day and my 'Big Band' show every Sunday. To date I have not sold sufficient time to exceed my draw.

ALAN B. SKUBA, MAYOR   JAMES M. RADY, MAYOR PRO TEM   DOUGLAS G. BEST   LORRAINE H. BOYCE   JERRY C. HARMON

Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
March 28, 1977
Page Two


"I have not received, nor will I receive, any compensation
for the Dickerson account."

In the first councilmanic action related hereto, i.e. approval
of the Tentative Map Act, Mr. Best abstained. The matter will
be returning to the City Council for action related to the
final map. Therefore, it is respectfully requested that the
Commission render an opinion based on the foregoing facts, as
to the legality of Mr. Best's taking part in the discussions
and voting on the matter of the Dickerson subdivision map when
it appears before the Council for the final decision.

Very truly yours,

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY


J. WIEGAND
City Attorney

JW:bw

sections.question: Whether a City Councilman employed as an Account Executive, Newscaster and Disc Jockey at radio station KOWN may participate in governmental decisions relating to a client of his employer (the Dickerson Company, a land developer that has purchased advertising time at KOWN and seeks City Council approval of a tentative subdivision map).
sections.conclusion: Based on the facts presented, assuming the matter will have no material financial effect on the Councilman's interest in KOWN or Palomar Broadcasters, Inc., Councilman Best may participate in City Council discussions and voting on matters related to the Dickerson subdivision map. The FPPC found no financial interest defined under the Political Reform Act that would disqualify him from participation.
sections.question_synthetic: May a city councilman employed by a radio station participate in voting on matters involving companies that are advertising clients of the station, where the councilman has declined direct compensation from the client's account?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the councilman may participate in the voting because he has no direct financial interest in the Dickerson Company decision, receives no compensation from that account, and the matter appears to have no material financial effect on his employment or interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter from City Attorney Wiegand (dated March 28, 1977) and the FPPC response letter from Delbert L. Spurlock (dated March 30, 1977). The text is partially degraded/OCR-corrupted in places, particularly in the conclusion section, but the key legal analysis and conclusion are discernible. Confidence reduced due to document quality and fragmented text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a City Councilman employed as an Account Executive, Newscaster and Disc Jockey at radio station KOWN may participate in governmental decisions relating to a client of his employer (the Dickerson Company, a land developer that has purchased advertising time at KOWN and seeks City Council approval of a tentative subdivision map).

CONCLUSION: Based on the facts presented, assuming the matter will have no material financial effect on the Councilman's interest in KOWN or Palomar Broadcasters, Inc., Councilman Best may participate in City Council discussions and voting on matters related to the Dickerson subdivision map. The FPPC found no financial interest defined under the Political Reform Act that would disqualify him from participation.

embedding.summary: The FPPC issued advice that a city councilman employed by a radio station may vote on matters involving the station's advertising clients, where the councilman declines direct compensation from those clients and has no other financial interest triggered by the decision.
